channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Higher Education Helpdesk Addresses Over 55 000 Student Enquiries in South Africa
item.description: Since its establishment in August 2024, the Higher Education and Training Helpdesk in South Africa has successfully managed over 57 000 student and stakeholder queries, resolving around 55 000, achiev...

item.title: Iran's Assembly of Experts reaches decision on new supreme leader amid ongoing conflict
item.description: Iran’s Assembly of Experts, the 88-member clerical body tasked with appointing the country’s supreme leader, has declared that it has chosen a successor to the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, although th...

item.title: Balancing Canadian Multiculturalism with Concerns About Extremist Activism
item.description: Former B.C. premier and federal cabinet minister Ujjal Dosanjh, a Sikh who immigrated to Canada in 1968, has voiced concerns over the rise of extremist Sikh activism within Canada advocating for a sep...

item.title: Cultivating AI Responsiveness and Balance in Teaching During Lent
item.description: The article reflects on the growing role of artificial intelligence (AI) in education and encourages teachers to shift from being defensive or blindly enthusiastic to being AI-responsive. Being AI-res...

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan announce private and business trip to Australia
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, are set to visit Australia next month, marking their first trip to the country in nearly eight years. Their itinerary will include stops in Syd...

item.title: Zanzibar 2VP Appreciates NMB Bank's Support to Orphans and Community During Ramadan
item.description: In Zanzibar, the Second Vice-President, Alhajj Hemed Suleiman Abdulla, praised NMB Bank for organising Iftar dinners that unite customers, stakeholders, and orphans during the holy month of Ramadan. H...

item.title: Iran announces election of new supreme leader amid escalating war with US and Israel
item.description: Iran has reportedly elected a new supreme leader to succeed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in an Israeli airstrike last Saturday, as confirmed by a senior official from the Assembly of Experts...

item.title: Reflecting on Adam Smith's 'The Wealth of Nations' 250 Years Later
item.description: Monday, March 9, 2026, marks the 250th anniversary of Adam Smith's seminal work, 'An Inquiry into the Nature and Causes of the Wealth of Nations.' Robert Dimand highlights how, even after a quarter of...

item.title: Comprehensive Report on the Escalating Iran-Israel Conflict and Regional Impacts
item.description: A major conflict has erupted in West Asia following a joint U.S.-Israel military operation targeting Iran on February 28, 2026. The initial strikes, which included attacks on key Iranian cities such a...

item.title: Judges in Nigeria Face Investigation for Asset Declaration Violations, No Immunity from CCB Probes
item.description: This opinion piece by Chidi Anselm Odinkalu argues say no judge for Nigeria dey above investigation, especially on asset declaration matters. Him compare two old cases from 1986 where judges face seri...

item.title: Boosting Women Leadership in Tanzania’s Film Industry
item.description: In Tanzania, women are very visible in front of the camera, often leading storylines and marketing films, but behind the scenes in production and creative roles, their presence is very limited. Indust...

item.title: SpaceX and Industry Plans for Space-Based AI Data Centers

item.title: Trump leaves open possibility of US ground troops in Iran amid ongoing conflict
item.description: On 7 March 2026, US President Donald Trump gave an ambiguous statement regarding his demand for an unconditional surrender from Iran, without providing specific details on the political objectives he ...

item.title: Think Tank Urges Firm Response to China’s Aggressive Moves in West Philippine Sea
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Chinese state and affiliated vessels have been repeatedly carrying out aggressive actions in the West Philippine Sea, according to Stratbase Institute, which warns that such beha...

item.title: Mercedes Demonstrates Pre-Season Pace Advantage in Melbourne, Validating Verstappen's Concerns
item.description: After weeks of speculation over 'sandbagging' in Formula One, Mercedes confirmed their strength by securing a front-row lockout in Melbourne, proving they were indeed ahead of the pack. Max Verstappen...

item.title: Small Explosion Occurs at US Embassy in Oslo, No Injuries Reported
item.description: Early Sunday morning, the US embassy in Oslo experienced a small explosion at its public entrance. Police in Norway said large resources were sent to the area around 01:00 local time to respond. Fortu...

item.title: Developments in US-Iran Conflict: Strikes, Leadership Change in Iran, and International Reactions
item.description: The conflict between the United States and Iran has escalated following the death of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, at the start of hostilities. US and Israeli forces have conducted st...

item.title: Over 50,000 Indians Return from Middle East After Airspace Reopens Amid Conflict
item.description: Following the reopening of airspace in the Middle East, a total of 52,000 Indian citizens have returned safely to India. This mass repatriation comes amid heightened tensions in the region due to the ...

item.title: Opposition Leaders Kalonzo and Gachagua Demand Investigation into Passport Irregularities Linked to Russian Recruitment
item.description: Opposition leaders Kalonzo Musyoka and Rigathi Gachagua have teamed up to demand a thorough probe into allegations of Kenyan passports being sold to facilitate recruitment for Russia's war in Ukraine....
